discussion_posts.name: panekk
discussion_posts.content: I believe the test case with positions `[[0,0],[0,1],[1,2],[1,2]]` is invalid. The problem description states "We may perform an add land operation which **turns the water** at position **into a land**", it says nothing about adding the land twice. Either this testcase (and similar, if any) should be removed, or the description should not mention turning water into a land.

discussion_posts.name: jumppo90
discussion_posts.content: Anybody without a union find solution, raise your hands!

discussion_posts.name: silverarc
discussion_posts.content: Union find by rank with path compression should be O(1) (technically alpha v, v being numbers of union find objects) operation for find and union, so should it technically be O(4 * len(positions) ) as you at most have 4 neighbors?

discussion_posts.name: ZQGao
discussion_posts.content: One little hint: The elements in `positions` are not unique...

discussion_posts.name: Msey
discussion_posts.content: Union find is the key to success :)

discussion_posts.name: djslim
discussion_posts.content: Initialize parent nodes as pointing to nothing rather than pointing to itself like in classic union find

discussion_posts.name: apolloydy
discussion_posts.content: I write me c++ solution using stranded union-find with "balance" feature (with a rank). It passed first time. However, after few days when I trying to solve this problem again. I got a TLE at the 1, 10000 test case. I have cope paste some of the top rank post int c++ language. they actually slow than mine. (and also got a TLE). I do not know why? I saw the Java solution with the same idea only takes 15ms.

discussion_posts.name: SR_94
discussion_posts.content: Could you give some pointers to the follow up question in the description?\n`Follow up: Could you solve it in time complexity O(k log(mn)), where k == positions.length?` ?

discussion_posts.content: Anyone with an O(k log(mn)) solution?
